column1: CEOs now make 296 times what a typical worker earns : CEO-to-worker compensation ratio, 1965–2013

Note: CEO annual compensation is computed using the options realized" compensation series for CEOs at the top 350 U.S. firms ranked by sales. Typical worker compensation is average compensation of production/nonsupervisory workers in the key industries of the firms included in the sample. "

Source: EPI analysis of data from Compustat's ExecuComp database, Bureau of Labor Statistics Current Employment Statistics, and Bureau of Economic Analysis NIPA tables Reproduced from Figure C in CEO Pay Continues to Rise as Typical Workers Are Paid Less
